review_summary: The transcript and unit summary provide enough grounded detail to generate 4 conceptual reinforcement questions about why lstms help gradient flow without needing a separate video clip.

question_bank.question: What architectural feature most directly explains why LSTMs improve gradient flow?
choices: The cell state includes an additive update path
They remove all nonlinearities from recurrence
They force every gate to equal 1
They train with beam search instead of SGD
question_bank.explanation: The core idea is not just gating by itself, but the additive memory path. Addition creates a more direct route for information and gradients than repeatedly multiplying everything through the same recurrence.

transcript_quotes: fixes the problem of gradient flow

question_bank.question: Why was the forget gate an important addition to the original LSTM idea?
choices: Because pure accumulation without forgetting becomes dysfunctional on long sequences
Because it makes the model exactly equivalent to a CNN
Because it guarantees perfect recall of every token forever
Because it removes the need for any hidden state
question_bank.explanation: A purely additive memory that only keeps accumulating content eventually becomes overloaded. The forget gate lets the model clear or reduce stale information so memory remains useful over long sequences.

question_bank.question: What happens in the special case where the forget gate is 1 and the input gate is 0?
choices: The cell state is copied forward unchanged
The hidden state becomes all zeros
The model is forced to forget everything
The decoder switches into bidirectional mode
question_bank.explanation: If the model fully keeps old memory and writes in no new content, the cell state simply passes forward as it was. That is the cleanest illustration of long-distance information preservation in an LSTM.

question_bank.question: Which later neural-network idea was presented as following the same direct-path intuition as LSTMs?
question_bank.explanation: The lecture connected LSTM memory paths to residual-style architectures. In both cases, direct connections help information and gradients move more easily through the model.
